spec_match.rationale: Exercises `reading-flow` and `reading-order` on a focus-trapped grid, surfacing the tab traversal order live.

interactive.rationale: Three selects drive layout and ordering; the focus walker reports each step on Tab/Shift+Tab.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index promises a tab-walker; the page delivers.

edge_cases.rationale: Missing: contenteditable elements, items with negative tabindex, nested reading-flow containers.

openQuestions.title: Add a tabindex=-1 lane
openQuestions.detail: How does reading-flow interact with items that opt out of tabbing? Add one item with tabindex=-1 and show the walker skips it.

openQuestions.title: Add a nested reading-flow case
openQuestions.detail: Spec allows nested reading-flow containers; the walker should descend in nested order. Build one example.

summary: Best ergonomic demo of the focus/AT impact — the walker readout is a strong teaching aid. Minor extension opportunities.
